1. What exists in packages/ai-tools (@belt/ai) - especially any Claude Code integration

3. Any existing WebSocket, terminal, or PTY-related code

{
  "name": "@belt/ai",
  "description": "Belt utilities to work with AI - AgentRC cascade rules for Claude Code",
  "type": "module",
  "sideEffects": false,
  "version": "0.1.2",
  "author": "Antonio Presto <antoniopresto@gmail.com>",
  "license": "MIT",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt",
    "directory": "packages/ai-tools"
  },
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/tree/main/packages/ai-tools#readme",
  "keywords": [
    "claude-code",
    "agentrc",
    "rules",
    "cascade",
    "hooks",
    "ai-tools"
  ],
  "main": "./dist/index.cjs",
  "module": "./dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "bin": {
    "belt-ai": "./dist/setup.mjs",
    "belt-ai-setup": "./dist/setup.mjs",
    "belt-ai-hook": "./dist/hook-runner.mjs"
  },
  "exports": {
    "./define": {
      "types": "./dist/define.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/define.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/define.cjs"
    },
    "./hook-runner": {
      "types": "./dist/hook-runner.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/hook-runner.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/hook-runner.cjs"
    },
    "./hooks": {
      "types": "./dist/hooks.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/hooks.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/hooks.cjs"
    },
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    },
    "./setup": {
      "types": "./dist/setup.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/setup.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/setup.cjs"
    },
    "./types": {
      "types": "./dist/types.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/types.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/types.cjs"
    }
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "vite build",
    "dev": "vite build --watch",
    "test": "vitest run --exclude '**/*.integration.test.ts' --exclude '**/*.e2e.test.ts' --passWithNoTests",
    "test:watch": "vitest --watch --exclude '**/*.integration.test.ts' --exclude '**/*.e2e.test.ts'",
    "test:integration": "vitest run --reporter=verbose src/__tests__/integration",
    "test:all": "vitest run",
    "coverage": "vitest --coverage",
    "check": "run-s check:*",
    "check:types": "tsc --noEmit",
    "check:lint": "eslint src --fix",
    "check:format": "prettier --write ."
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@belt/tools": "workspace:*"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@belt/prettier-plugin": "workspace:*",
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.2",
    "@types/bun": "^1.3.5",
    "@types/lodash": "^4.17.21",
    "@types/node": "^25.0.3",
    "@types/react": "^19.2.7",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react-swc": "^4.2.2",
    "eslint": "^9.39.2",
    "npm-run-all": "^4.1.5",
    "prettier": "^3.7.4",
    "prettier-plugin-organize-imports": "^4.3.0",
    "rollup-plugin-node-externals": "^8.1.2",
    "terser": "^5.44.1",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.52.0",
    "vite": "^7.3.0",
    "vite-plugin-dts": "^4.5.4",
    "vite-plugin-externalize-deps": "^0.10.0",
    "vite-tsconfig-paths": "^6.0.3",
    "vitest": "^4.0.16"
  }
}

{
  "name": "@belt/tools",
  "description": "TypeScript utility library with runtime type validation, state management, and common helpers",
  "type": "module",
  "sideEffects": false,
  "version": "0.1.2",
  "author": "Antonio Presto <antoniopresto@gmail.com>",
  "license": "MIT",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt",
    "directory": "packages/tools"
  },
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/tree/main/packages/tools#readme",
  "keywords": [
    "typescript",
    "validation",
    "state-management",
    "schema",
    "runtime-types",
    "helpers",
    "utilities"
  ],
  "main": "./dist/index.cjs",
  "module": "./dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    },
    "./schema": {
      "types": "./dist/schema.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/schema.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/schema.cjs"
    },
    "./state-machine": {
      "types": "./dist/state-machine.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/state-machine.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/state-machine.cjs"
    }
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "vite build",
    "dev": "vite build --watch",
    "test": "vitest run --passWithNoTests",
    "test:watch": "vitest --watch --passWithNoTests",
    "coverage": "vitest --coverage",
    "check": "run-s check:*",
    "check:types": "tsc --noEmit",
    "check:lint": "eslint src --fix",
    "check:format": "prettier --write ."
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "date-fns": "^4.1.0",
    "immer": "^11.1.3",
    "lodash": "^4.17.21",
    "lru-cache": "^11.2.4",
    "minimatch": "^10.1.1",
    "typebox": "^1.0.73"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@belt/prettier-plugin": "workspace:*",
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.2",
    "@types/lodash": "^4.17.21",
    "@types/node": "^25.0.3",
    "@types/react": "^19.2.7",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react-swc": "^4.2.2",
    "eslint": "^9.39.2",
    "npm-run-all": "^4.1.5",
    "prettier": "^3.7.4",
    "prettier-plugin-organize-imports": "^4.3.0",
    "rollup-plugin-node-externals": "^8.1.2",
    "terser": "^5.44.1",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.52.0",
    "vite": "^7.3.0",
    "vite-plugin-dts": "^4.5.4",
    "vite-plugin-externalize-deps": "^0.10.0",
    "vite-tsconfig-paths": "^6.0.3",
    "vitest": "^4.0.16"
  }
}

1→# @belt/cli
     2→
     3→Project scaffolding and AI harness for Claude Code. Create production-ready monorepos with built-in AI brainstorming using local LLMs.
     4→
     5→## Installation
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→# Global installation (recommended)
     9→bun install -g @belt/cli
    10→
    11→# Or use npx
    12→npx @belt/cli init ./my-project
    13→```
    14→
    15→**Requirements:**
    16→
    17→- Bun >= 1.0.0
    18→- Ollama (optional, for brainstorming features)
    19→
    20→## Features
    21→
    22→- **Project Scaffolding** - Create monorepo structures with Turborepo, TypeScript, and Biome
    23→- **AgentRC Setup** - Configure Claude Code hooks with cascade rules
    24→- **AI Brainstorming** - Generate feature backlogs using Self-MoA and Self-Refine patterns
    25→- **Local LLM Integration** - Use Ollama models for routing and analysis
    26→- **Progress Persistence** - Track session state and feature development
    27→
    28→---
    29→
    30→## Quick Start
    31→
    32→### Create a New Monorepo
    33→
    34→```bash
    35→belt init ./my-project
    36→```
    37→
    38→This creates a complete monorepo with:
    39→
    40→- `package.json` with Turborepo configuration
    41→- `turbo.json` with build/test pipelines
    42→- `tsconfig.json` with TypeScript strict mode
    43→- `.gitignore` with common patterns
    44→- `.belt/` directory for harness state
    45→- `.claude/settings.json` with hook configuration
    46→- `.agentrc.ts` with security rules
    47→- `CLAUDE.md` with AI assistant instructions
    48→
    49→### Setup AgentRC in Existing Project
    50→
    51→```bash
    52→belt setup
    53→# or
    54→belt setup ./my-project
    55→```
    56→
    57→Creates the `.belt/`, `.claude/`, and `.agentrc.ts` files for Claude Code integration.
    58→
    59→### Run AI Brainstorming
    60→
    61→```bash
    62→# Interactive mode
    63→belt brainstorm
    64→
    65→# Non-interactive mode
    66→belt brainstorm -n \
    67→  --name "MyApp" \
    68→  --description "A task management app" \
    69→  --domain "productivity" \
    70→  --goal "Track daily tasks" \
    71→  --goal "Share tasks with team"
    72→```
    73→
    74→Generates a feature backlog using:
    75→
    76→1. **Context Phase** - Gather project information
    77→2. **Diverge Phase** - Generate ideas from multiple personas (Self-MoA)
    78→3. **Judge Phase** - Evaluate features by relevance, feasibility, value
    79→4. **Refine Phase** - Improve top features (Self-Refine)
    80→5. **Synthesize Phase** - Group into epics and prioritize
    81→6. **Review Phase** - User approval of final backlog
    82→
    83→---
    84→
    85→## Commands
    86→
    87→| Command                 | Description                               |
    88→| ----------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
    89→| `belt init [dir]`       | Initialize new monorepo with full harness |
    90→| `belt setup [dir]`      | Setup AgentRC in existing project         |
    91→| `belt hook`             | Hook runner for Claude Code (stdin)       |
    92→| `belt brainstorm [dir]` | Run AI brainstorming session              |
    93→
    94→### Global Options
    95→
    96→```
    97→--help, -h         Show help message
    98→--version, -v      Show version number
    99→```
   100→
   101→### Brainstorm Options
   102→
   103→```
   104→--non-interactive, -n  Run without interactive prompts
   105→--name <name>          Project name
   106→--description <desc>   Project description
   107→--domain <domain>      Project domain
   108→--goal <goal>          Project goal (repeatable)
   109→--ollama-host <url>    Ollama API URL (default: http://localhost:11434)
   110→```
   111→
   112→---
   113→
   114→## Harness System
   115→
   116→The harness provides AI-powered development assistance using local LLMs.
   117→
   118→### Required Ollama Models
   119→
   120→| Model                 | Role                   | Size  |
   121→| --------------------- | ---------------------- | ----- |
   122→| `phi4-mini-reasoning` | Task routing           | 2.5GB |
   123→| `deepseek-r1:8b`      | Reasoning, analysis    | 5GB   |
   124→| `qwen2.5-coder:7b`    | Code review, debugging | 5GB   |
   125→
   126→Models are automatically pulled when running `belt init` or `belt brainstorm`.
   127→
   128→### Agents
   129→
   130→| Agent             | Model            | Capabilities                   |
   131→| ----------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------------ |
   132→| `project-manager` | deepseek-r1:8b   | Backlog management, priorities |
   133→| `tech-lead`       | deepseek + qwen  | Architecture, code review      |
   134→| `product-owner`   | deepseek-r1:8b   | Requirements, user stories     |
   135→| `code-reviewer`   | qwen2.5-coder:7b | Code analysis, suggestions     |
   136→
   137→### Persistence
   138→
   139→Files in `.belt/harness/`:
   140→
   141→| File                      | Purpose                                |
   142→| ------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
   143→| `progress.txt`            | Session log with decisions and actions |
   144→| `features.json`           | Feature backlog with status tracking   |
   145→| `brainstorm-state.json`   | Brainstorm session state for recovery  |
   146→| `brainstorm-backlog.json` | Generated feature backlog              |
   147→| `rollback/`               | Session snapshots for recovery         |
   148→
   149→---
   150→
   151→## Programmatic API
   152→
   153→### Harness Exports
   154→
   155→```typescript
   156→import {
   157→  // Ollama Client
   158→  OllamaClient,
   159→  createOllamaClient,
   160→  checkRequiredModels,
   161→
   162→  // Task Router
   163→  TaskRouter,
   164→  createRouter,
   165→
   166→  // Agents
   167→  AGENTS,
   168→  executeAgent,
   169→  streamAgent,
   170→  collaborativeAnalysis,
   171→
   172→  // Brainstorm
   173→  BrainstormSession,
   174→  createBrainstormSession,
   175→  runBrainstorm,
   176→  BRAINSTORM_PERSONAS,
   177→
   178→  // Persistence
   179→  initBeltDirectory,
   180→  createProgressFile,
   181→  appendProgress,
   182→  addFeature,
   183→  updateFeatureStatus,
   184→} from '@belt/cli/harness';
   185→```
   186→
   187→### Commands Exports
   188→
   189→```typescript
   190→import { init, setup, hook, brainstorm } from '@belt/cli/commands';
   191→
   192→// Run commands programmatically
   193→await init('./my-project');
   194→await setup('./existing-project');
   195→```
   196→
   197→### Utils Exports
   198→
   199→```typescript
   200→import {
   201→  discoverEntries,
   202→  generateExports,
   203→  updatePackageJson,
   204→} from '@belt/cli/utils';
   205→
   206→// Use in vite.config.ts
   207→const entries = discoverEntries('./src');
   208→```
   209→
   210→### Templates Exports
   211→
   212→```typescript
   213→import {
   214→  generateClaudeMd,
   215→  generateAgentrc,
   216→  generateMonorepoFiles,
   217→} from '@belt/cli/templates';
   218→```
   219→
   220→---
   221→
   222→## Example: Using the Brainstorm API
   223→
   224→```typescript
   225→import {
   226→  createBrainstormSession,
   227→  type ProjectContext,
   228→} from '@belt/cli/harness';
   229→
   230→const context: ProjectContext = {
   231→  name: 'TaskMaster',
   232→  description: 'A collaborative task management application',
   233→  domain: 'productivity',
   234→  goals: [
   235→    'Track individual and team tasks',
   236→    'Set deadlines and reminders',
   237→    'Generate productivity reports',
   238→  ],
   239→};
   240→
   241→const session = createBrainstormSession({
   242→  projectDir: './my-project',
   243→  context,
   244→  ollamaHost: 'http://localhost:11434',
   245→  interactive: false,
   246→});
   247→
   248→// Run full pipeline
   249→const backlog = await session.run();
   250→
   251→console.log(`Generated ${backlog.epics.length} epics`);
   252→console.log(`Total features: ${backlog.features.length}`);
   253→```
   254→
   255→---
   256→
   257→## Example: Using Agents
   258→
   259→```typescript
   260→import {
   261→  createOllamaClient,
   262→  executeAgent,
   263→  streamAgent,
   264→  collaborativeAnalysis,
   265→} from '@belt/cli/harness';
   266→
   267→const client = createOllamaClient('http://localhost:11434');
   268→
   269→// Execute single agent
   270→const response = await executeAgent(client, 'tech-lead', {
   271→  task: 'Review this API design',
   272→  context: 'REST API for user management',
   273→});
   274→
   275→// Stream agent response
   276→for await (const chunk of streamAgent(client, 'code-reviewer', {
   277→  task: 'Analyze this function for bugs',
   278→  code: functionCode,
   279→})) {
   280→  process.stdout.write(chunk);
   281→}
   282→
   283→// Collaborative analysis (multiple agents)
   284→const analysis = await collaborativeAnalysis(client, {
   285→  task: 'Design authentication system',
   286→  agents: [
   287→    'tech-lead',
   288→    'product-owner',
   289→  ],
   290→});
   291→```
   292→
   293→---
   294→
   295→## Example: Task Routing
   296→
   297→```typescript
   298→import { createRouter, createOllamaClient } from '@belt/cli/harness';
   299→
   300→const client = createOllamaClient();
   301→const router = createRouter(client);
   302→
   303→const result = await router.route('Implement OAuth2 login flow');
   304→
   305→console.log(result.category); // 'code' | 'reasoning' | 'simple' | 'cloud'
   306→console.log(result.model); // Recommended model for the task
   307→console.log(result.reason); // Explanation of routing decision
   308→```
   309→
   310→---
   311→
   312→## Generated Project Structure
   313→
   314→After running `belt init ./my-project`:
   315→
   316→```
   317→my-project/
   318→├── .belt/
   319→│   ├── harness/
   320→│   │   ├── progress.txt
   321→│   │   ├── features.json
   322→│   │   └── rollback/
   323→│   ├── config/
   324→│   └── temp/
   325→├── .claude/
   326→│   └── settings.json
   327→├── packages/
   328→├── .agentrc.ts
   329→├── .gitignore
   330→├── CLAUDE.md
   331→├── package.json
   332→├── tsconfig.json
   333→└── turbo.json
   334→```
   335→
   336→---
   337→
   338→## Philosophy
   339→
   340→> "Better spend 3 days creating a robust setup than weeks recreating
   341→> projects because the harness starts hallucinating as the project grows."
   342→

# @belt/cli

Project scaffolding and AI harness for Claude Code. Create production-ready monorepos with built-in AI brainstorming using local LLMs.

## Installation

```bash
# Global installation (recommended)
bun install -g @belt/cli

# Or use npx
npx @belt/cli init ./my-project
```

**Requirements:**

- Bun >= 1.0.0
- Ollama (optional, for brainstorming features)

## Features

- **Project Scaffolding** - Create monorepo structures with Turborepo, TypeScript, and Biome
- **AgentRC Setup** - Configure Claude Code hooks with cascade rules
- **AI Brainstorming** - Generate feature backlogs using Self-MoA and Self-Refine patterns
- **Local LLM Integration** - Use Ollama models for routing and analysis
- **Progress Persistence** - Track session state and feature development

---

## Quick Start

### Create a New Monorepo

```bash
belt init ./my-project
```

This creates a complete monorepo with:

- `package.json` with Turborepo configuration
- `turbo.json` with build/test pipelines
- `tsconfig.json` with TypeScript strict mode
- `.gitignore` with common patterns
- `.belt/` directory for harness state
- `.claude/settings.json` with hook configuration
- `.agentrc.ts` with security rules
- `CLAUDE.md` with AI assistant instructions

### Setup AgentRC in Existing Project

```bash
belt setup
# or
belt setup ./my-project
```

Creates the `.belt/`, `.claude/`, and `.agentrc.ts` files for Claude Code integration.

### Run AI Brainstorming

```bash
# Interactive mode
belt brainstorm

# Non-interactive mode
belt brainstorm -n \
  --name "MyApp" \
  --description "A task management app" \
  --domain "productivity" \
  --goal "Track daily tasks" \
  --goal "Share tasks with team"
```

Generates a feature backlog using:

1. **Context Phase** - Gather project information
2. **Diverge Phase** - Generate ideas from multiple personas (Self-MoA)
3. **Judge Phase** - Evaluate features by relevance, feasibility, value
4. **Refine Phase** - Improve top features (Self-Refine)
5. **Synthesize Phase** - Group into epics and prioritize
6. **Review Phase** - User approval of final backlog

---

## Commands

| Command                 | Description                               |
| ----------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| `belt init [dir]`       | Initialize new monorepo with full harness |
| `belt setup [dir]`      | Setup AgentRC in existing project         |
| `belt hook`             | Hook runner for Claude Code (stdin)       |
| `belt brainstorm [dir]` | Run AI brainstorming session              |

### Global Options

```
--help, -h         Show help message
--version, -v      Show version number
```

### Brainstorm Options

```
--non-interactive, -n  Run without interactive prompts
--name <name>          Project name
--description <desc>   Project description
--domain <domain>      Project domain
--goal <goal>          Project goal (repeatable)
--ollama-host <url>    Ollama API URL (default: http://localhost:11434)
```

---

## Harness System

The harness provides AI-powered development assistance using local LLMs.

### Required Ollama Models

| Model                 | Role                   | Size  |
| --------------------- | ---------------------- | ----- |
| `phi4-mini-reasoning` | Task routing           | 2.5GB |
| `deepseek-r1:8b`      | Reasoning, analysis    | 5GB   |
| `qwen2.5-coder:7b`    | Code review, debugging | 5GB   |

Models are automatically pulled when running `belt init` or `belt brainstorm`.

### Agents

| Agent             | Model            | Capabilities                   |
| ----------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------------ |
| `project-manager` | deepseek-r1:8b   | Backlog management, priorities |
| `tech-lead`       | deepseek + qwen  | Architecture, code review      |
| `product-owner`   | deepseek-r1:8b   | Requirements, user stories     |
| `code-reviewer`   | qwen2.5-coder:7b | Code analysis, suggestions     |

### Persistence

Files in `.belt/harness/`:

| File                      | Purpose                                |
| ------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `progress.txt`            | Session log with decisions and actions |
| `features.json`           | Feature backlog with status tracking   |
| `brainstorm-state.json`   | Brainstorm session state for recovery  |
| `brainstorm-backlog.json` | Generated feature backlog              |
| `rollback/`               | Session snapshots for recovery         |

---

## Programmatic API

### Harness Exports

```typescript
import {
  // Ollama Client
  OllamaClient,
  createOllamaClient,
  checkRequiredModels,

  // Task Router
  TaskRouter,
  createRouter,

  // Agents
  AGENTS,
  executeAgent,
  streamAgent,
  collaborativeAnalysis,

  // Brainstorm
  BrainstormSession,
  createBrainstormSession,
  runBrainstorm,
  BRAINSTORM_PERSONAS,

  // Persistence
  initBeltDirectory,
  createProgressFile,
  appendProgress,
  addFeature,
  updateFeatureStatus,
} from '@belt/cli/harness';
```

### Commands Exports

```typescript
import { init, setup, hook, brainstorm } from '@belt/cli/commands';

// Run commands programmatically
await init('./my-project');
await setup('./existing-project');
```

### Utils Exports

```typescript
import {
  discoverEntries,
  generateExports,
  updatePackageJson,
} from '@belt/cli/utils';

// Use in vite.config.ts
const entries = discoverEntries('./src');
```

### Templates Exports

```typescript
import {
  generateClaudeMd,
  generateAgentrc,
  generateMonorepoFiles,
} from '@belt/cli/templates';
```

---

## Example: Using the Brainstorm API

```typescript
import {
  createBrainstormSession,
  type ProjectContext,
} from '@belt/cli/harness';

const context: ProjectContext = {
  name: 'TaskMaster',
  description: 'A collaborative task management application',
  domain: 'productivity',
  goals: [
    'Track individual and team tasks',
    'Set deadlines and reminders',
    'Generate productivity reports',
  ],
};

const session = createBrainstormSession({
  projectDir: './my-project',
  context,
  ollamaHost: 'http://localhost:11434',
  interactive: false,
});

// Run full pipeline
const backlog = await session.run();

console.log(`Generated ${backlog.epics.length} epics`);
console.log(`Total features: ${backlog.features.length}`);
```

---

## Example: Using Agents

```typescript
import {
  createOllamaClient,
  executeAgent,
  streamAgent,
  collaborativeAnalysis,
} from '@belt/cli/harness';

const client = createOllamaClient('http://localhost:11434');

// Execute single agent
const response = await executeAgent(client, 'tech-lead', {
  task: 'Review this API design',
  context: 'REST API for user management',
});

// Stream agent response
for await (const chunk of streamAgent(client, 'code-reviewer', {
  task: 'Analyze this function for bugs',
  code: functionCode,
})) {
  process.stdout.write(chunk);
}

// Collaborative analysis (multiple agents)
const analysis = await collaborativeAnalysis(client, {
  task: 'Design authentication system',
  agents: [
    'tech-lead',
    'product-owner',
  ],
});
```

---

## Example: Task Routing

```typescript
import { createRouter, createOllamaClient } from '@belt/cli/harness';

const client = createOllamaClient();
const router = createRouter(client);

const result = await router.route('Implement OAuth2 login flow');

console.log(result.category); // 'code' | 'reasoning' | 'simple' | 'cloud'
console.log(result.model); // Recommended model for the task
console.log(result.reason); // Explanation of routing decision
```

---

## Generated Project Structure

After running `belt init ./my-project`:

```
my-project/
├── .belt/
│   ├── harness/
│   │   ├── progress.txt
│   │   ├── features.json
│   │   └── rollback/
│   ├── config/
│   └── temp/
├── .claude/
│   └── settings.json
├── packages/
├── .agentrc.ts
├── .gitignore
├── CLAUDE.md
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── turbo.json
```

---

## Philosophy

> "Better spend 3 days creating a robust setup than weeks recreating
> projects because the harness starts hallucinating as the project grows."

The Belt CLI enforces:

- **Production-ready code** - No mocks, full tests required
- **Strict TypeScript** - Proper typing throughout
- **Incremental commits** - Conventional commit format
- **AI guardrails** - Restrictive rules to prevent hallucination

---

## License

MIT

# Belt Project - Senior Research Report

## Executive Summary

This document consolidates research findings for preparing Belt monorepo for NPM publishing with Claude Code automation. The term "Anthropic Harness" refers to Anthropic's agent harness architecture documented in their engineering blog, specifically the patterns for building long-running AI agents that persist work across context windows.

---

## 1. Anthropic Harness Architecture

### What It Is

The "harness" is the orchestration layer that manages agent execution, context, and state persistence. Anthropic's Claude Agent SDK implements this pattern.

### Core Problem Solved

Long-running agents lose context between sessions. Each new context window starts blank. The harness provides mechanisms for agents to:
- Resume work from previous sessions
- Track progress across multiple sessions
- Avoid duplicate work or premature completion claims

### Two-Agent Pattern

1. **Initializer Agent** (first session):
   - Creates `init.sh` for environment setup
   - Writes `claude-progress.txt` for human-readable state
   - Generates comprehensive feature list (JSON format)
   - Establishes git baseline

2. **Coding Agent** (subsequent sessions):
   - Follows startup ritual (pwd, read progress, review features)
   - Implements one feature per session
   - Updates progress file and commits changes
   - Runs verification tests

### Key Artifacts

| Artifact | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `claude-progress.txt` | Human-readable progress summary |
| `features.json` | Structured requirements (200+ items) |
| `init.sh` | Environment initialization |
| Git history | Technical change record |

### Failure Patterns to Avoid

1. **Premature completion** - Agent declares "done" without verification
2. **Context exhaustion** - Trying too much in single session
3. **Lost state** - Poor handoff documentation between sessions
4. **Scope drift** - Agent removes tests or requirements

---

## 2. Claude Code Hooks (Official Documentation)

### Configuration Location

```
~/.claude/settings.json          # User-level
.claude/settings.json            # Project-level (version controlled)
```

### Available Events

| Event | Description | Matcher Support |
|-------|-------------|-----------------|
| PreToolUse | Before tool execution | Yes |
| PostToolUse | After tool completion | Yes |
| PermissionRequest | Permission dialog shown | Yes |
| UserPromptSubmit | User submits prompt | No |
| Stop | Agent finishes | No |
| SubagentStop | Subagent finishes | No |
| SessionStart | Session begins | startup/resume/clear/compact |
| SessionEnd | Session ends | clear/logout/exit/other |
| PreCompact | Before context compaction | manual/auto |

### Hook Schema

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "EventName": [
      {
        "matcher": "ToolPattern",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "shell-command",
            "timeout": 60
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Exit Codes

- `0` - Success (output processed)

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the full exploration report:

## COMPREHENSIVE BELT MONOREPO EXPLORATION REPORT

Based on my systematic exploration of the belt monorepo, here's what currently exists and how it's architected:

---

### **1. CURRENT PACKAGES OVERVIEW**

#### **@belt/tools** (Core Utilities)
- **Status**: Foundation library, well-established
- **Key Components**:
  - Schema validation using TypeBox with runtime type checking
  - Immer-based state machine for reactive state management
  - Helpers: date, string, object, error utilities
  - LRU Cache, minimatch support
- **Architecture**: Modular exports (schema, state-machine, helpers)
- **No WebSocket/PTY code** - purely utility-focused

#### **@belt/ai** (Claude Code Integration)
- **Status**: Hook system for Claude Code automation
- **Key Components**:
  - `defineAgent()` - Type-safe agent configuration
  - `.agentrc.ts` cascade rules system
  - `hook-runner.ts` - CLI tool that intercepts Claude Code hooks via stdin
  - Pre/post-tool hooks with rule matching (string, regex, async functions)
  - Context injection capabilities
- **Integration Method**: stdin-based hook runner (no WebSocket)
- **Exit Codes**: 0 (allow), 2 (block), 1 (error)
- **Target Hook Events**: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, Stop, etc.

#### **@belt/cli** (AI Harness & Scaffolding)
- **Status**: Comprehensive harness system - this is the main orchestration layer
- **Key Infrastructure**:
  
  **Ollama Integration** (`harness/ollama.ts`):
  - Client for local LLM inference
  - Models: phi4-mini-reasoning, deepseek-r1:8b, qwen2.5-coder:7b
  - Chat & generate endpoints with streaming support
  - Model management (pull, list, check availability)
  
  **Task Router** (`harness/router.ts`):
  - Routes tasks to appropriate models based on classification
  - Categories: code, reasoning, simple, cloud
  - Uses phi4-mini-reasoning with fallback heuristics
  - Confidence scoring
  
  **Agents** (`harness/agents/index.ts`):
  - Pre-defined personas: project-manager, tech-lead, product-owner, code-reviewer
  - Each has specific system prompt and model assignment
  - `executeAgent()` - single agent execution
  - `streamAgent()` - async generator for streaming responses
  - `collaborativeAnalysis()` - multi-agent sequential analysis
  - Context accumulation across agent responses
  
  **Brainstorm System** (`harness/brainstorm/`):
  - Full pipeline: Context → Diverge → Judge → Refine → Synthesize → Review
  - Self-MoA (Mixture of Agents) pattern for feature generation
  - Self-Refine loops with LLM-as-Judge for evaluation
  - Six distinct phases with persona assignments
  - Session state persistence
  
  **Terminal UI** (`harness/progress-ui.ts`):
  - ANSI escape codes for multi-progress bar display
  - Download progress visualization
  - Speed calculation and formatting
  - TTY detection with fallback for non-interactive
  - **Uses**: Cursor movement (`\x1b[nA/B`), clearing (`\x1b[2K`), colors
  
  **Download Manager** (`harness/download-manager.ts`):
  - Parallel Ollama model pulling
  - Concurrent request limiting (p-limit)
  - Progress tracking per model
  - EventEmitter pattern (allStarted, progress, completed, error, retry)
  
  **Persistence** (`harness/persistence/progress.ts`):
  - `.belt/harness/` directory structure
  - progress.txt (human-readable log)
  - features.json (structured backlog)
  - brainstorm-state.json (session recovery)
  - rollback snapshots
  
  **No WebSocket Implementation** - All integration is HTTP/REST with Ollama

#### **@belt/compliance** (Daemon Infrastructure)
- **Status**: Process management and system integration
- **Key Components**:
  - `ComplianceDaemon` class - async event loop with setTimeout
  - PID file management
  - Log rotation (5MB limit)
  - Launchd integration (macOS)
  - Signal handling (SIGINT, SIGTERM)
  - robotjs for system input (accessibility features)
- **Pattern**: Long-running daemon with graceful shutdown

---

### **2. EXISTING WEBSOCKET/PTY/TERMINAL PATTERNS**

**Status**: None exist currently
- No `websocket` or `socket.io` dependencies
- No `pty.js` or `xterm.js` imports
- No child_process spawning for pseudo-terminals
- Terminal output uses standard `process.stdout.write()` with ANSI codes
- Only browser-based testing via Playwright MCP

**Existing Terminal Capabilities**:
- ANSI progress bars (progress-ui.ts)
- Command execution via Bash tool
- CLI argument parsing with commander.js
- Log file management

---

### **3. CLAUDE CODE INTEGRATION ARCHITECTURE**

**Current Setup** (from `.claude/settings.json`):
- Hook system via `.agentrc.ts` cascade rules
- Pre-commit hooks running `bun lint`
- Pre-push hooks running `bun run build && bun run typecheck`
- Conventional commit validation
- Permission deny/allow lists for tools

**Hook Runner Flow**:
```
Claude Code Tool Execution
  ↓
Reads stdin (JSON with tool_name, tool_input, file_path)
  ↓
Locates .agentrc.ts files (cascade up directory tree)
  ↓
Evaluates rules + PreToolUse hook
  ↓
Block (exit 2) | Warn (stderr) | Allow (exit 0)
  ↓
Inject context if needed
```

**Invocation Method**: `bunx @belt/ai hook` (no WebSocket)

---

### **4. STATE MANAGEMENT & PERSISTENCE PATTERNS**

**Session State Handling** (brainstorm system):
- `BrainstormSession` class with event emitters
- Session phases as async functions with results accumulation
- State saved to JSON between phases
- Rollback snapshots in `.belt/harness/rollback/`

**Event-Driven Architecture**:
- EventEmitter pattern in download-manager
- Listener registration for progress events
- No central event bus (localized per manager)

**Daemon Pattern** (compliance):
- setInterval/setTimeout loops
- PID file for process tracking
- Signal handlers for graceful shutdown
- Log rotation

---

### **5. TECH STACK & DEPENDENCIES**

**Core Stack**:
- Bun (runtime + package manager)
- Turborepo (monorepo orchestration)
- TypeScript 5.9.3 (strict mode)
- Biome (linting/formatting)
- Vitest (testing)

**Key Libraries**:
- TypeBox (schema validation)
- Immer (immutable state)
- date-fns (date utilities)
- p-limit (concurrency control)
- commander (CLI argument parsing)
- robotjs (system automation - macOS only)

**No WebSocket/PTY Deps**: Not installed

---

### **6. ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS THAT COULD BE LEVERAGED**

**For Custom Claude Code Terminal Interface**:

1. **Hook System** (already exists)
   - Use `.agentrc.ts` to inject terminal commands
   - PreToolUse hook to capture output

2. **Agent Architecture** (ready to extend)
   - Add new agent type for terminal operations
   - Reuse system prompt pattern

3. **Progress UI Pattern** (proven ANSI approach)
   - Multi-state terminal rendering
   - TTY detection fallback

4. **Session Persistence** (working model)
   - State accumulation in JSON
   - Rollback snapshots
   - Progress logging

5. **Daemon Pattern** (from compliance)
   - Long-running event loop
   - Process lifecycle management
   - Log rotation

6. **Event-Driven Architecture**
   - EventEmitter pattern for status updates
   - Hook-based callbacks

---

### **7. WHAT'S MISSING FOR TERMINAL INTERFACE**

To build a custom Claude Code terminal interface, you'd need to add:

- **Communication Layer**:
  - WebSocket server (or stdio-based streaming)
  - Message protocol (JSON or binary)
  - Connection state management

- **PTY Management**:
  - node-pty or similar for shell spawning
  - Signal forwarding (SIGWINCH for resize)
  - Output buffering/streaming

- **Terminal Emulation**:
  - xterm.js for browser display (if browser-based)
  - Ansii parser for output formatting
  - Color/style preservation

- **State Synchronization**:
  - Two-way sync between terminal and session state
  - Command history persistence
  - Output capture and indexing

---

### **8. RECOMMENDED INTEGRATION POINTS**

If building terminal interface as **new package** (`@belt/terminal`):

1. **Extend Agent System**:
   ```typescript
   // New agent type for terminal commands
   TerminalAgent {
     id: 'terminal-executor',
     capabilities: ['command-execution', 'output-streaming']
   }
   ```

2. **Leverage Existing Persistence**:
   - Use `.belt/harness/terminal/` for session state
   - Add terminal-specific backlog type

3. **Reuse Hook Pattern**:
   - Agent registration in `.agentrc.ts`
   - PreToolUse hook for command validation

4. **Build on Progress UI**:
   - Command execution progress indicator
   - Output streaming with ANSI preservation

5. **Use State Machine** from @belt/tools:
   - Terminal session states (idle, running, error)
   - Proper transitions and recovery

---

### **ABSOLUTE FILE PATHS TO KEY FILES**

**Core Infrastructure**:
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/cli/src/harness/ollama.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/cli/src/harness/router.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/cli/src/harness/agents/index.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/cli/src/harness/brainstorm/session.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/cli/src/harness/progress-ui.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/cli/src/harness/download-manager.ts`

**Claude Code Integration**:
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/ai-tools/src/hook-runner.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/ai-tools/src/types/agent.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/.claude/settings.json`

**State Management**:
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools/src/state-machine/index.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/cli/src/harness/persistence/progress.ts`

**Daemon Pattern Reference**:
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/compliance/src/daemon.ts`

---

### **SUMMARY**

The belt monorepo is **production-grade infrastructure for AI-assisted development** with strong emphasis on:
- Session persistence and recovery
- Multi-agent collaborative systems
- Local LLM orchestration
- Claude Code integration via rules/hooks
- Robust error handling and state management

**No WebSocket/PTY exists**, but the architecture is modular enough to add these as separate packages that leverage existing patterns. The harness is designed as CLI-first, not browser/terminal UI-first.
